inline.body:
<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Symlink-following behavior silently dropped in `prepareOutDir.ts` — The original `copyDir` used `fs.statSync` (which follows symlinks) to dispatch, so symlinks to directories were transparently dereferenced and their contents copied recursively. The replacement `fs.cpSync` with only `recursive: true` and no `dereference: true` copies symlinks as bare symlinks (Node's `dereference` defaults to `false`). This is a real behavioral regression for any `publicDir` containing symlinks.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Symlink-following behavior silently dropped in `create-vite/index.ts` — Same issue as `prepareOutDir.ts`: the original `copyDir`/`copy` pair in create-vite used `fs.statSync` to follow symlinks. The replacement `fs.cpSync` call omits `dereference: true`, causing symlinks in template directories to be copied as bare symlinks instead of being dereferenced.

sticky:
<!-- squarewright:review -->

## Squarewright review

This PR violates an explicit, precedence-taking project rule (`.review-rules/copy.md`) that prohibits swapping `copyDir` for `fs.cpSync`, and introduces a real behavioral regression: `dereference: true` is omitted from the `fs.cpSync` calls, so symlinks in `publicDir` and template directories will be copied as bare symlinks instead of being dereferenced. Additionally, a defensive self-copy guard is removed without replacement.

**4** finding(s): 🔴 3  ·  🟡 1  ·  🔵 0

- 🔴 `packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts:1` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Violates `.review-rules/copy.md` and `CONVENTIONS.md` — prohibited swap of `copyDir` for `fs.cpSync` — The trusted, precedence-taking rule in `.review-rules/copy.md` explicitly requires recursive/directory copies to go through the project's `copyDir` helper and states that a change removing or swapping `copyDir` for `fs.cpSync` must be flagged. `CONVENTIONS.md` repeats this requirement. This PR deletes `copyDir` from `utils.ts` and replaces all call sites with direct `fs.cpSync` calls in all three changed files, doing precisely the prohibited thing.
- 🔴 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Symlink-following behavior silently dropped in `prepareOutDir.ts` — The original `copyDir` used `fs.statSync` (which follows symlinks) to dispatch, so symlinks to directories were transparently dereferenced and their contents copied recursively. The replacement `fs.cpSync` with only `recursive: true` and no `dereference: true` copies symlinks as bare symlinks (Node's `dereference` defaults to `false`). This is a real behavioral regression for any `publicDir` containing symlinks.
- 🔴 `packages/create-vite/src/index.ts:647` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Symlink-following behavior silently dropped in `create-vite/index.ts` — Same issue as `prepareOutDir.ts`: the original `copyDir`/`copy` pair in create-vite used `fs.statSync` to follow symlinks. The replacement `fs.cpSync` call omits `dereference: true`, causing symlinks in template directories to be copied as bare symlinks instead of being dereferenced.
- 🟡 `packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts:1` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Self-copy guard removed without replacement — The deleted `copyDir` implementation had a defensive guard (`if (srcFile === destDir) continue`) that prevented infinite/erroneous copies when the destination directory is nested inside the source. `fs.cpSync` has no analogous skip. While unlikely to trigger in practice for the current `prepareOutDir` use case (outDir and publicDir are usually siblings), the removal is a latent correctness risk.

---
_Reviewed by: Correctness, Security · glm-5.2 · findings reflect the enabled lenses only_

unplaceable.message: Violates `.review-rules/copy.md` and `CONVENTIONS.md` — prohibited swap of `copyDir` for `fs.cpSync` — The trusted, precedence-taking rule in `.review-rules/copy.md` explicitly requires recursive/directory copies to go through the project's `copyDir` helper and states that a change removing or swapping `copyDir` for `fs.cpSync` must be flagged. `CONVENTIONS.md` repeats this requirement. This PR deletes `copyDir` from `utils.ts` and replaces all call sites with direct `fs.cpSync` calls in all three changed files, doing precisely the prohibited thing.

Self-copy guard removed without replacement — The deleted `copyDir` implementation had a defensive guard (`if (srcFile === destDir) continue`) that prevented infinite/erroneous copies when the destination directory is nested inside the source. `fs.cpSync` has no analogous skip. While unlikely to trigger in practice for the current `prepareOutDir` use case (outDir and publicDir are usually siblings), the removal is a latent correctness risk.
